title: Mildly Winey Brazilian Ume Plum Onigiri
description: A unique Brazilian dish featuring ume plum and wine, shaped like nigiri and served on a talavera plate, perfect for a brunch mood. This sugar-free friendly dish is a must try for those looking for a twist on traditional Brazilian cuisine.
instructions: Rinse the Japanese short grain rice thoroughly and soak it in water for 30 minutes.
Drain the water and cook the rice with 1 cup of water and 1/4 teaspoon of salt in a medium saucepan over low heat.
In a small saucepan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat and add the diced ume plum, cooking until it is lightly caramelized.
Add the white wine to the saucepan with the ume plum and cook until the liquid is almost completely reduced.
Once the rice is cooked, allow it to cool down to room temperature.
Mix the cooked ume plum with the cooled rice.
Prepare a small bowl of water for shaping the onigiri.
Using your hands, shape the rice mixture into small triangular shapes, moistening your hands with water as needed.
Wrap each onigiri with a noru sheet.
Serve the onigiri on a talavera plate.
